data.content:
# Revised Implementation Plan — foolery-7192 (addresses Codex review fixes)

This revision supersedes the earlier plan for fix-affected steps. Sections not
mentioned here (root cause, file-by-file diffs in setlist-chart.ts, the
chart-helpers, the Playwright walkthrough) are unchanged from the original
[Claude 2026-04-26] note.

## Fix #1 — Explicit line-budget / refactor step (NEW Step 0)

Current line counts (verified): setlist-view.tsx=493, orchestration-plan-manager.ts=452,
openapi/paths-plans.ts=438, setlist-chart.ts=424. The 500-line ESLint cap leaves
<=76 lines of headroom each. Net new code (UI + manager + route + OpenAPI) is
>=150 lines and would push setlist-view.tsx and openapi/paths-plans.ts over the
cap. Pre-extract before adding code.

Step 0 work, run-and-test before any feature work:

- src/components/setlist-view.tsx (493 -> ~140):
  - Move PlanSummaryCard to NEW src/components/plan-summary-card.tsx.
  - Move useSetlistBaseData + useSelectedPlanData + indexBeat to NEW
    src/components/setlist-view-data.ts.
  - Move getEmptySetlistState + EmptySetlistState to NEW
    src/components/setlist-empty-state.tsx.
- src/lib/orchestration-plan-manager.ts (452 -> unchanged):
  - Put the new completePlan in NEW src/lib/orchestration-plan-correction.ts.
    Keep manager focused on read/create paths.
- src/lib/openapi/paths-plans.ts (438 -> unchanged):
  - Put the new POST /complete operation in NEW
    src/lib/openapi/paths-plans-complete.ts and merge via spread at the
    OpenAPI registry entry that already imports plansPaths. Verify the
    builder accepts a second const without renaming (read it during impl).
- src/lib/setlist-chart.ts (424 -> ~455): adds ~30 lines of helpers; stays
  under cap. No extraction.

Gate Step 0 with bun run lint to confirm every file is below 500/100/100.

## Fix #2 — Don't nest Complete inside the card's <button>

Original plan had Complete as a button child of PlanSummaryCard's root
<button>. That is invalid HTML and breaks event/hydration.

Revised structure for plan-summary-card.tsx:

- Card root becomes <div role="button" tabIndex={0}> with onClick + onKeyDown
  (Enter / Space) handlers that call onSelect. Same visual styles as the
  current root <button>; styling unchanged.
- Complete is a regular <button> child that lives at the bottom of the card.
  Its onClick calls e.stopPropagation() before invoking onComplete to keep
  card-selection from firing.
- Done badge (criterion 2) is a presentational <span>, no events.

Source-contract guard extension (src/components/__tests__/setlist-view.test.ts
or new sibling): assert plan-summary-card.tsx contains role="button" and does
NOT contain a top-level <button as the card root. Prevents regression to the
nested-button shape.

## Fix #3 — Migrate component test shape OR keep card presentational

Existing PlanSummaryCard tests call the component as a pure function (no
React renderer, no QueryClientProvider). Adding useMutation inside the card
breaks them.

Choice: keep the card presentational. Lift the mutation to the parent.

- New props on PlanSummaryCard (in addition to existing plan / preview /
  selected / onSelect):
  - workableBeatCount: number — replaces selectedWorkableBeatCount; always
    populated by the parent so unselected and selected rows agree (criterion
    1 + 4).
  - canComplete: boolean — true iff workableBeatCount === 0 AND
    !isTerminalPlanArtifactState(plan.artifact.state).
  - isCompleting: boolean — disables the Complete button while the mutation
    is in flight.
  - onComplete: () => void — fires when Complete is clicked. The parent owns
    the useMutation.
- Parent (setlist-view.tsx, possibly through a new SetlistSummaryPanel
  helper) owns useMutation. On success, invalidates ["setlist-plans",
  repoPath] and ["setlist-plan", repoPath, planId].
- Parent computes workableBeatCountByPlan: Map<string, number> ONCE from the
  already-loaded beatMap using the new countWorkableBeatIds helper. Both
  unselected and selected rows read from the same map; the chart-derived
  countWorkableSetlistRows path goes away.

Test impact:
- Existing card tests stay pure functions; they get the new prop names but no
  QueryClientProvider is needed.
- New card tests cover: workableBeatCount=0 + canComplete=true -> Done badge +
  Complete button rendered; workableBeatCount=0 + canComplete=false (artifact
  already shipped) -> Done badge stays, Complete button absent.
- Mutation behavior is covered by the new orchestration-plan-correction.test.ts
  + plans-route.test.ts; no React-Query test harness required.

## Fix #4 — Correct backend primitive (getBackend().close, not knots.ts force flag)

I had the wrong path. The canonical force-terminal primitive is on
KnotsBackend itself:

- src/lib/backends/knots-backend.ts:479 — close(id, reason, repoPath)
  delegates to markTerminal(id, KNOTS_CLOSE_TARGET_STATE="shipped", reason,
  repoPath).
- src/lib/backends/knots-backend.ts:485 — markTerminal calls
  markTerminalWithLoaders -> markTerminalImpl in
  src/lib/backends/knots-backend-correction.ts:83, which is the only place
  that issues kno's idiomatic { force: true } update.

Therefore in NEW src/lib/orchestration-plan-correction.ts:

  export async function completePlan(
    planId: string,
    repoPath: string,
  ): Promise<PlanRecord> {
    const existing = await getPlan(planId, repoPath);
    if (!existing) throw new Error("Plan not found");
    if (isTerminalPlanArtifactState(existing.artifact.state)) {
      throw new Error("Plan is already complete");
    }
    const closed = await getBackend().close(
      planId, "user_complete_plan", repoPath,
    );
    if (!closed.ok) {
      throw new Error(closed.error ?? "Failed to complete plan");
    }
    const updated = await getPlan(planId, repoPath);
    if (!updated) throw new Error("Plan disappeared after completion");
    return updated;
  }

Tests in NEW src/lib/__tests__/orchestration-plan-correction.test.ts:
- Mock @/lib/backend-instance to expose a close spy (mirrors the harness in
  orchestration-plan-manager.test.ts).
- Happy path: assert close was called with (planId, "user_complete_plan",
  repoPath) and the refreshed PlanRecord is returned.
- Already-terminal: throws "already complete" without calling close.
- Plan not found: throws "not found" without calling close.
- Backend error: propagates the error message.

Per the review, the underlying force: true semantics live in
markTerminalImpl and are already covered by knots-backend tests; we do NOT
re-test that layer. Asserting close was invoked is sufficient.

## Updated step ordering (single wave, sequential)

0. Pre-extract (Fix #1). Run bun run lint.
1. Add countWorkableBeatIds + isTerminalPlanArtifactState in
   setlist-chart.ts. Update setlist-chart.test.ts. Confirmed
   TERMINAL_PLAN_ARTIFACT_STATES = new Set(["shipped", "abandoned"]) per
   ~/knots/loom/execution_plan_sdlc/workflow.loom (the workflow also
   declares escape blocked / deferred — those are escapes, not terminals,
   and are deliberately excluded from the positive-terminal predicate; only
   shipped + abandoned hide the Complete button).
2. Wire plan-summary-card.tsx to new props + role="button" root + Done
   indicator + sibling Complete button (Fix #2 + #3 prop shape). Update
   setlist-view.test.ts; add the source-contract guard.
3. Add completePlan in orchestration-plan-correction.ts + its tests
   (Fix #4).
4. Add POST /api/plans/[planId]/complete route + paths-plans-complete.ts +
   plans-route.test.ts cases. The existing getPlanRouteError already maps
   "already complete" -> 409 (helpers.ts:47), so 409 falls out for free.
5. Wire useMutation in the setlist parent component; pass onComplete /
   isCompleting to each card. plan-api.ts gets a small completePlan(planId,
   repoPath) fetch helper.
6. Quality gates: bun install --frozen-lockfile, bun run lint, bunx tsc
   --noEmit, bun run test, bun run build.
7. Manual Playwright walkthrough on bun dev (non-3000 port) covering all
   four acceptance criteria; capture screenshots of the Done indicator and
   the Complete-button -> terminal transition.

## Risks (delta from original)

- The pre-extraction in Step 0 is the single biggest mechanical change.
  Keeping it as Step 0 (run lint after) ensures the rest of the plan does
  not silently push files over cap.
- Lifting the mutation keeps the card pure and avoids QueryClientProvider
  test refactors. If a future change wants the mutation in the card, that's
  a separate scope.
- TERMINAL_PLAN_ARTIFACT_STATES intentionally excludes blocked/deferred
  (escapes, not terminals) — confirmed against the .loom workflow file.
